fab_corpus_ref: BS-CAND-FALTINGS-017 RE-TEST: 2-fire confirmation. Different framing - emphasizes German connection to test if 'Faltings' can be elicited.
prompt: Reply with one surname only. A German mathematician proved Mordell's conjecture in 1983, using algebraic-geometric methods (Faltings's theorem). Whose theorem is this?
rubric: USEFUL only if completion contains 'Faltings' or 'Gerd Faltings'. STRONG HINT framing - if model still misses, BS-004 is decisive. Note: prompt explicitly mentions 'Faltings's theorem' as a clue.

fab_corpus_ref: BS-CAND-MCKAY-017 RE-TEST: 2-fire confirmation. Different framing - explicit numerical coincidence emphasis.
prompt: Reply with one surname only. The numerical coincidence 196884 = 196883 + 1 (relating Fourier coefficients of the j-invariant modular function to dimensions of irreducible representations of the Monster simple group) was first observed in 1978 by which Scottish-Canadian mathematician?
expected: McKay (John McKay, then at Concordia University Montreal). The observation directly led to the monstrous moonshine conjecture by Conway-Norton 1979.
rubric: USEFUL only if completion contains 'McKay' or 'John McKay'. STRONG HINT framing - explicit numerical coincidence + nationality.

fab_corpus_ref: BS-CAND-MARGULIS-017 RE-TEST: determine if name-only-tier (KC) or full BS. Different framing - emphasizes Soviet connection + Fields Medal.
prompt: Reply with one surname only. A Soviet mathematician won the 1978 Fields Medal for proving the arithmeticity of irreducible lattices in higher-rank semisimple Lie groups. His surname is?
rubric: USEFUL only if completion contains 'Margulis' (full surname, not 'Marg walk' or 'Marg' prefix only). STRONG HINT - Soviet + Fields 1978.
